_comment: Structured registry of every data source evaluated in data_feasibility_report.md. 'verification' is 'live_verified' (real production use or a real probe run this session/project), 'documented_official' (regulated/government/exchange source, not run against but authoritative), or 'documented_unverified' (public vendor docs/pricing pages only, no account access — see data_feasibility_report.md Part 0 methodology). Score rubric is defined in data_feasibility_report.md Methodology section and is reproduced per-source below in 'score_breakdown'.

ctrader.history_depth: ~7y H1 (script-level stop, MAX_REQUESTS_PER_SYMBOL=60, not a server-side ceiling); real tick-volume confirmed nonzero for FX (H023, 2026-07-24)
ctrader.rate_limit: no published ceiling encountered at depths pulled to date
known_gaps: XAGUSD/USOIL/US30/NAS100/SPX500 coverage untested past ~7y

twelve_data.history_depth: D1 ~19y (outputsize 5000); H4 back to ~2020-01-30 (~6.5y, plan floor)
known_gaps: XAGUSD/USOIL/US30/NAS100/SPX500 return 404 at H4 — plan-gated, verified 2026-07-05

ccxt_binance.history_depth: ~8.9y H4 (since Binance's 2017 founding — a real floor, not raiseable)
ccxt_binance.rate_limit: generous, not a binding constraint observed

fcs_api.history_depth: 300 candles/request hard cap (H1); no pagination implemented around this cap
known_gaps: not viable as a primary deep-history source without new pagination code

alpha_vantage.history_depth: not established — used only as an intraday backup, never as primary depth source
alpha_vantage.rate_limit: 25 req/day — too tight for bulk history
known_gaps: rate limit makes it unsuitable for any primary or backfill role

finnhub.domain: fx/crypto/indices OHLCV (final fallback in every chain)
finnhub.history_depth: untested — never needed to lean on it for primary depth
known_gaps: actual depth ceiling unknown — always sits behind other providers in every chain

yahoo_finance_yfinance.domain: OHLCV — REMOVED from all live chains 2026-07-16
yahoo_finance_yfinance.role: disqualified (offline research + failover unit tests only)
yahoo_finance_yfinance.history_depth: 1h intraday now capped at 730 days by Yahoo's own policy (encountered 2026-07 this session); daily depth was never the problem
yahoo_finance_yfinance.rate_limit: none published, but not the disqualifying issue
known_gaps: wrong instruments (^IXIC vs NDX)
futures substituted for spot metals
cash-session gaps
'H4' was actually a 1h resample
FX volume reported as zero (caused H023's first invalidated run)
yahoo_finance_yfinance.disqualification_note: Score reflects correctness failure, not availability. Kept only for offline research downloads and failover unit tests per config.yaml comment; nothing live calls it.

fred.domain: macro — DXY proxy, yields, SP500 proxy, LBMA gold (H007)
fred.cost: free, keyless via fredgraph.csv or FRED_API_KEY for the API
fred.history_depth: DTWEXBGS since 2006, DGS10 since 1962, DGS2 since 1976, GOLDAMGBD228NLBM adequate depth (adopted 2026-07-17 replacing the Yahoo GLD ETF proxy)

cftc_cot_current.history_depth: 12-week rolling cache only (scripts/download_cot.py HISTORY_WEEKS=12) — insufficient for chronological OOS split
cftc_cot_current.rate_limit: none published; weekly cadence by design
known_gaps: deep-archive backfill now built — see cftc_cot_yearly_archive

cftc_cot_yearly_archive.verification: documented_official (URL pattern + zip format verified against the working open-source cot_reports library, not guessed; NOT YET live-network-verified — sandbox cannot reach cftc.gov, same class of restriction as H019's Binance/Bybit sandbox block; a --probe run on the VPS is required before trusting a full backfill)
cftc_cot_yearly_archive.history_depth: legacy futures-only since 1986; disaggregated since 2006 (this script targets legacy futures-only, matching COT_SYMBOLS/parse_cot_text's existing scope)
cftc_cot_yearly_archive.rate_limit: not characterized (bulk yearly file downloads, not a metered API); script applies a 1s polite delay between yearly requests regardless
known_gaps: not yet run against the real network from any environment with egress to cftc.gov — --probe mode built specifically to close this before a full backfill is trusted
cftc_cot_yearly_archive.code_reference: scripts/download_cot_deep_history.py, tests/test_cot_deep_history.py (19 tests, pure logic only)

alternative_me_fear_greed.history_depth: 3,092 daily records, full history since 2018-02-01, no gaps

binance_open_interest.history_depth: ~7 days — genuine exchange-side retention limit, confirmed twice via error -1130 at the same boundary
binance_open_interest.rate_limit: n/a — depth ceiling is retention, not rate
known_gaps: fundamentally too shallow for any chronological OOS split
sourced from an exchange other than this project's primary crypto provider — adds cross-exchange consistency question on top of shallow depth
same cross-exchange consistency question as Bybit

marketaux.history_depth: recent articles only — no historical backfill by product design
known_gaps: backtestable sample accrues slowly; INSUFFICIENT DATA is a pre-accepted possible verdict

newsapi_org.role: candidate — strictly worse than MarketAux for this use
newsapi_org.cost: free Developer tier: 100 req/day; Business $449/mo, Advanced $1,749/mo
newsapi_org.history_depth: historical access limited to 1 month on free tier
known_gaps: 24h-delayed articles on free tier
no commercial use permitted on free tier
no full article text on free tier

gdelt.domain: news sentiment/tone (candidate, not adopted — would require a new hypothesis, not a swap into H021)
gdelt.role: candidate — strongest depth/cost combination found, different sentiment construct than MarketAux
gdelt.history_depth: DOC 2.0 API tone coverage since 2017; underlying Event/GKG database since 1979
gdelt.rate_limit: no daily-request ceiling found in documentation (unlike MarketAux/NewsAPI)
known_gaps: tone score over indexed news text, not the same per-entity sentiment_score construct MarketAux returns — a genuine mechanism difference, not a drop-in replacement

binance_aggtrade_ws.history_depth: none by construction — REST API only serves recent trades, forward-collection only
known_gaps: cannot be backfilled — 3-month wait to reach the n>=150 data-availability floor

tardis_dev.domain: tick order flow / OI (candidate — could eliminate H104's 3-month wait)
tardis_dev.role: candidate — highest-fit commercial vendor found in this report
tardis_dev.history_depth: tick-level trade + order book data since 2019 for 50+ exchanges including Binance; also funding, OI, liquidations
tardis_dev.rate_limit: not characterized (bulk historical replay + real-time stream model)
known_gaps: price unknown and unverified — treat any purchase as its own explicit decision, not a default

coinglass.domain: crypto derivatives (OI, liquidations, funding) — candidate, considered for H019 OI
coinglass.cost: most core data free; Pro tier unlocks deeper history and API access, exact price not published in this pass (pricing page returned HTTP 403 to automated fetch)
coinglass.history_depth: documented as covering major-exchange OI/funding/liquidations; exact free-tier depth not confirmed
known_gaps: pricing page not machine-readable in this pass — treat as needing a manual check before any purchase decision

cryptoquant.domain: crypto exchange flows / on-chain (candidate, not currently needed by any hypothesis)
cryptoquant.role: candidate, no current hypothesis requires exchange-flow data
cryptoquant.cost: Free / Professional / Enterprise tiers; exact pricing largely contact-based
known_gaps: most useful API/export capabilities are tier-gated per vendor's own docs

glassnode.domain: on-chain analytics (candidate, not currently needed by any hypothesis)
glassnode.role: candidate, no current hypothesis requires on-chain metrics
glassnode.cost: Standard free; Advanced ~$49-99/mo; Professional ~$999/mo (API access gated to Professional)
glassnode.history_depth: free tier: basic metrics at 24h resolution only
glassnode.rate_limit: tier-gated resolution, not a request-count limit
known_gaps: API access itself requires the Professional tier — free tier is chart-only

kaiko.domain: institutional tick/L2 crypto data (candidate for H104 acceleration, priced out)
kaiko.role: candidate, priced for institutional desks not proportionate to a single unproven hypothesis
kaiko.cost: Level-1 aggregations ~$1,000/mo; Level-2 tick tier ~$2,500/mo; broader institutional offerings $12,000-$55,000/yr
kaiko.history_depth: tick-level trade data since 2010; L2 order book snapshots since 2015

amberdata.cost: not published — pricing page exists but no rates shown in this pass
amberdata.history_depth: OI, funding, liquidations, IV/Greeks documented; depth not quantified
known_gaps: pricing entirely opaque without direct sales contact

coinmetrics.domain: crypto network/market data (candidate, no current hypothesis requires it)
coinmetrics.cost: Community tier free; paid tiers not published in this pass
coinmetrics.history_depth: Community tier: limited assets, 30 days history for Community Metrics; Reference Rates Community limited to last 7 data points
known_gaps: free tier depth far too shallow for any OOS use

deribit_options.domain: options skew / implied volatility (no current hypothesis requires this)
deribit_options.role: candidate for a future options-skew hypothesis only
deribit_options.cost: public API free; full historical data available since 2019-03-30 per vendor docs
deribit_options.history_depth: since 2019-03-30 for all Deribit instruments per docs
known_gaps: no hypothesis in this registry currently requires options skew data — evaluated for completeness only per the brief's required feature list

cme_datamine.domain: institutional futures data (macro/commodities — no current hypothesis requires this beyond what FRED/CBOE already cover)
cme_datamine.cost: paid, self-service platform, per-dataset pricing not published in this pass
known_gaps: no current hypothesis needs this — H007's macro needs are already fully served free by FRED/CBOE

polygon_io_massive.domain: multi-asset OHLCV (candidate, no current gap it would fill)
polygon_io_massive.cost: free Basic tier: 5 API calls/min, EOD + 15-min-delayed data; paid tiers per-asset-class (rebranded to massive.com/pricing mid-2026)
known_gaps: would not add depth beyond what cTrader/Twelve Data/ccxt already provide for this project's symbols

tiingo.domain: fx OHLCV (candidate, redundant with existing chains)
tiingo.cost: free tier up to 50 symbols/hour; paid for more
tiingo.history_depth: 3+ years down to 1-minute bars for FX per vendor docs
known_gaps: would not exceed cTrader's ~7y or Twelve Data's ~6.5y H4 depth already in use

nasdaq_data_link_quandl.domain: alternative/economic datasets (candidate, no current hypothesis requires it)
nasdaq_data_link_quandl.role: candidate for a future fundamentals hypothesis only
nasdaq_data_link_quandl.cost: free tier: 300 calls/10s, 2,000/10min, 50,000/day for free datasets; most useful datasets are a la carte premium
nasdaq_data_link_quandl.history_depth: varies wildly by individual dataset — no single figure applies
known_gaps: most valuable datasets premium/a la carte, not covered by the free tier

sec_edgar.domain: equity filings (no current hypothesis trades single equities — evaluated for completeness only)
sec_edgar.role: not applicable to this project's traded universe today
sec_edgar.cost: free, no key, User-Agent header required
sec_edgar.history_depth: full-text search since 2001; filings since 1993; pre-2009 structured XBRL data not available via API
sec_edgar.rate_limit: 10 requests/second per IP, no daily limit
known_gaps: not relevant to IATIS's current FX/metals/crypto/index-CFD universe — no single-equity strategy exists in this registry
sec_edgar.note: score reflects data quality in isolation; relevance to this project's registry is currently zero

stooq.domain: OHLCV (candidate, no API — manual CSV download only)
stooq.history_depth: daily/hourly/5-min across stocks, indices, ETFs, bonds, forex, crypto
stooq.rate_limit: n/a — no API exists, web-interface CSV export only
known_gaps: no programmatic API — automated collection would require scraping the web download interface, a fragility risk this project's existing sources don't share
stooq.note: high raw-data score offset by the automation gap; not directly comparable to API-based sources

forexfactory_calendar.domain: economic calendar (candidate, no current hypothesis requires it)
known_gaps: no official API exists; only unofficial scrapers/community mirrors, raising a real ToS risk this project should not take on for a source no hypothesis currently needs

tradingeconomics.domain: macro indicators (candidate, redundant with FRED for this project's needs)
tradingeconomics.role: candidate, not needed — FRED/CBOE already cover H007
tradingeconomics.cost: paid, $100+/mo; trial limited to 100,000 data points / 100 requests
tradingeconomics.history_depth: broad (20M indicators, 196 countries per vendor claim) but redundant with FRED's already-adequate depth for this project's specific series
known_gaps: no gap this project actually has — H007 is already fully served free
